reasoning: The Tax Reform Bills, introduced by President Tinubu, include a provision to exempt certain real estate transactions from VAT. The bills have strong executive backing and have successfully passed the House of Representatives as of March 13, 2025. The primary remaining hurdles are Senate concurrence and presidential assent. Northern opposition, which previously threatened the bills, largely dissipated in January 2025 when a revised VAT-sharing formula was recommended. The Senate, having resumed from recess in late April / early May, is explicitly prioritizing the bills. With nearly two full months between the knowledge cutoff (May 5, 2025) and the resolution date (July 1, 2025), there is adequate time for the Senate to pass the legislation and for the President (who strongly supports his own administration's initiative) to sign it. While there is a non-trivial risk of legislative delays—such as potential dragged-out debates or the need for a conference committee to reconcile House and Senate versions—the momentum and priority status of the legislation make enactment before July 1 highly probable, estimated at 72%.
